title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Demott Cluttercuck and his superior discuss the suspicious circumstances surrounding the poisoning of Heather Bardwell. The crime occurred during an afternoon event where a prescription drug was administered via a cocktail, a risky method given the presence of twenty to thirty people. While the local detective, Conish, noted that Heather's husband, Mr. Bardcock, appeared tense, he concluded the couple seemed loyal. Demott decides to take Tidler, a movie enthusiast, to investigate the case.

Detective Moore (also referred to as Dermot Clark) visits Miss Marple to inform her that Scotland Yard has taken over the murder case. Miss Marple reflects on Heather's character, noting that while she was a good housekeeper, she was not particularly considerate, which may have left her husband lonely. Miss Marple suggests that the detectives visit Lady Bunty, who lives in a cottage at Gosington Manor and attended the party. She advises them to mention "Charlotte's Daughters" to Lady Bunty to prompt relevant information.

Later, Demott Clark meets Mrs. Bantry. She observes that while there is no official evidence regarding how the poison was administered, the community treats it as a murder. However, she finds it difficult to imagine a motive, stating that although Heather was an unpleasant, overly emotional, and over-the-top woman, she does not believe anyone would want to kill her.
